trades.market: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth from Q4 2026 through Q4 2027
trades.catalyst: The July 30 advance estimate for Q2 2026 real GDP will update the growth trajectory and may reduce recession pricing if expansion remains positive.
trades.risk_notes: A prolonged restrictive-rate environment, commodity shock, or sharp labor-market deterioration could produce consecutive negative quarters. The contract uses a mechanical GDP definition rather than an NBER recession determination and includes Q4 2026.
trades.grounding_source: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis, National Income and Product Accounts Table 1.1.1 and Gross Domestic Product release: https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product

avoid.reason: The zero-cut and at-least-one-cut markets are approximately complementary, and the supplied data do not establish an independent edge of at least 8% after policy uncertainty.
The adjacent CPI thresholds imply substantial probability mass at the reported 3.4% value, but no component-level CPI data or independent forecast is supplied to justify an 8% edge.
The GDP threshold ladder appears internally coherent, while no independent nowcast inputs are available to support a qualifying edge.
These contracts fail the $50,000 minimum-volume filter.

notes: Market pricing indicates an inflationary regime: elevated July inflation probabilities, high commodity-price thresholds, and an approximately 82.6% implied probability of no 2026 Fed cuts. The signal is market-derived and should be treated cautiously without contemporaneous component data.
